Also, a paired t-test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.001$</tex> shows that these improvements are statistically significant, making AMCNN a strong and scalable way to do sentiment analysis in recommendation systems.

title: DGLA-Net: Robust MRI-PET Fusion Method Based on Deep Global-Local Attention and Multi-Scale Supervision
authors: Ruichen Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: MRI-PET image fusion aims to integrate anatomical and functional information, which is crucial for precise diagnosis. However, existing deep learning methods struggle to balance texture details with functional fidelity, often facing bottlenecks such as difficulty in bridging the semantic gap, interference from background noise, and the blurring of critical lesions. This paper proposes an improved model, DGLA-Net, based on the UNet++ architecture and a dual robust information fusion attention mechanism (DGLA), aimed at optimizing fusion performance through deep global-local attention. The model utilizes a Nested UNet (UNet++) as the backbone architecture, leveraging its dense nested skip connections to effectively bridge the semantic gap between the encoder and decoder, thereby preserving multi-scale anatomical structural features to the maximum extent. Building on this, the core innovations include three aspects: First, a Deep Global-Local Attention (DGLA) module is designed to be highly compatible with UNet++ multi-level feature paths. By coupling global pooling with local convolution operations, it realizes dynamic screening and interaction of cross-modal features, balancing global context dependency with local texture details. Second, a Residual Denoising Spatial Attention (RDSA) module is introduced, utilizing residual connection mechanisms to strengthen deep feature extraction capabilities and effectively suppress background noise in functional images. Finally, an Adaptive Hybrid Loss function is constructed, synergistically integrating gradient loss, SSIM loss, and PET saliency-weighted intensity loss. Extensive experiments on MRI-PET multi-modal datasets demonstrate that DGLA-Net significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art fusion algorithms. Compared to other models, DGLA-Net not only retains brain anatomical contours more clearly but also locates tumor metabolic regions more precisely through attention heatmaps, effectively achieving an optimal balance between structural fidelity and functional saliency, demonstrating excellent potential for clinical application.

title: Flexible Graphene Tactile Sensors with Spider Web Designs for Human-Computer Interaction
authors: Brandon Singleton, Teja V. S. N. Vuddagiri, Juan A. Zepeda Maldonado, Mose Williams, Sandip Das
abstract: Laser Induced graphene (LIG) is an emerging technology that facilitates the production of multilayer graphene sheets and has found applications in various types of electronic sensors. Here, we present a set of three innovative spider web-shaped tactile sensors fabricated using LIG electrodes on flexible polyimide (PI) substrates. A 450 nm near-UV diode laser was used to directly form conductive spider web graphene electrodes and their connection traces on PI films. Raman spectroscopy and optical microscopy confirmed the formation of multilayer graphene under optimized laser irradiation conditions. By interfacing the graphene electrodes with an ATmega328P microcontroller and front-end electronics, functional capacitive touch sensors were realized. The spider web electrode geometry provided wide-area coverage while maintaining electrical conductivity and mechanical compliance under bending, making these flexible tactile sensors suitable for human-machine interaction (HMI) applications.

title: Domain-Adaptive Named Entity Recognition for Engineering Topology Texts
authors: Longqiang Zuo, Xi Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To address the challenges of diverse entity types, ambiguous boundaries, and dense domain-specific terminology in topology-oriented technical texts, this paper proposes a deep learning - based information extraction model that integrates domain-adaptive pretraining with a vocabulary enhancement mechanism, termed NetNER. Specifically, the BERT model is further pretrained on topology-related corpora to strengthen domain-aware semantic representations, while a vocabulary boundary - aware mechanism is introduced to improve the recognition of long and composite entities. A CRF decoding layer is then employed to ensure the consistency of label sequences. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach serves as a critical enabling technology for the automated configuration and intelligent management of complex engineering systems.

title: Mesdasd: A Medical Expert System for Diagnosing Autoimmune System Diseases
authors: Mohammadreza Hajiarbabi
abstract: Expert systems are valuable tools in various fields, including control, diagnosis, and medical applications. In recent decades, expert systems have been increasingly used in the medical field. Immune system diseases encompass a wide range of conditions, affecting approximately 8% of the U.S. population. These diseases target different parts of the body, including neurons, tissues, and skin. Some immune disorders share similar symptoms, while others resemble unrelated conditions, making diagnosis challenging even for specialized physicians. An expert system can assist not only the patients but also the physicians in the diagnostic process by leveraging artificial intelligence. For designing MESDASD (Medical Expert System for Diagnosing Autoimmune System Diseases), forward chaining method was used. For inference, certainty theory was applied. By using certainty theory the rules which are the symptoms of the diseases will have different certainty factors, that shows those with higher certainty factor are more important in the process of decision making. Fourteen tests, two for each diseases in moderate and severe case were used to evaluate the system. The system managed to pass all tests correctly with high margin. The results has demonstrated that MESDASD can accurately diagnose these diseases with a high level of certainty. MESDASD is the first expert system designed to diagnose various immune system diseases. In its initial version, the system primarily focuses on immune disorders affecting the skin and other closely related conditions. The system passed all the tests with high certainty.

title: Inflections Points in Quantum Computing as an Information Technology: A Thematic Analysis
authors: Nathan Jeffrey Elrod
abstract: Information Technology (IT) as a research discipline observes phenomena at the intersection of users, technology and information, and examines the needs and solutions that connect them. Quantum computing is a rapidly advancing field, and its state as an Information Technology at present is not well-understood. This study seeks to enhance the understanding of quantum computing as an Information Technology by identifying inflection points in its history and development through a series of expert interviews. Experts from two commercial quantum hardware/software developers, two academic institutions, and a United States National Lab were interviewed, and ten major historical themes relevant to quantum IT were identified in all. Ten inflection points were developed from these themes and specific historical trends were identified and discussed.

title: Generative Adversarial Network Based Anomaly Detection for Software Defect Prediction
authors: Aradhana Soni, James Ostrowski, Anahita Khojandi
abstract: Predicting software defects effectively is crucial for high-quality software development. Traditionally, defect prediction has relied on supervised techniques, limited by data availability. This paper applies a new Generative Adversarial Network-based technique for detecting anomalies (GAN-AN), tailored to early defect identification in software development. Our technique, which detects defects as anomalies, is compared against three established methods: autoencoder, isolation forest, and one-class support vector machine (OCSVM). Our experimentation, utilizing five datasets from the NASA Promise repository, shows GAN-AN to be superior, with improvements of 7 % in precision-recall area under the curve (PR-AUC) and a significant 20 % in F1 score over previous techniques. Further evaluation using validation datasets reveals that while GAN-AN's overall performance was not uniformly superior, it notably outperformed isolation forest and OCSVM in recall on several datasets. This underscores GAN-AN's potential in specific contexts where recall is critical and highlights the importance of context-specific model selection. Our investigation also reveals that performance metrics vary among models, suggesting that the most effective model may vary depending on the dataset and specific detection goals.

title: Accelerating Enterprise Application Development Through AI-Enhanced Low-Code Platforms: An Empirical Analysis of Oracle APEX with Generative AI Integration
authors: Mohankrishna Kothapalli, Dharanidhar Vuppu
abstract: Low-code platforms promise faster application delivery, but rigorous empirical evidence remains scarce. We conducted exploratory multi-study experiments with 12 professional developers and 20 business analysts to evaluate Oracle APEX with Select AI integration. Our findings reveal substantial productivity gains: developers completed equivalent functionality significantly faster than with ReactJS (mean speedup <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$37 \times$</tex>, though this ratio reflects both platform abstraction and AI benefits), writing 95 % less code. When we tested Select AI's natural language query capabilities across 1,250 queries, GPT4 achieved 71 % accuracy while Cohere and Oracle's models reached 64 % and 61 % respectively. Security testing with 140 adversarial prompts showed Row-Level Security blocked 96% of attacks. Business analysts with no Structured Query Language (SQL) training completed 78 % of data access tasks successfully using natural language alone. These exploratory results suggest AI-enhanced low-code platforms offer significant potential for enterprise development, warranting larger-scale replication studies.

title: Deterministic Kinematic Synchronization for High-Speed Manipulation of Non-Rigid Substrates
authors: Santosh R. Yadav
abstract: The automated manipulation of non-rigid substrates—such as textiles and flexible films—presents a fundamental challenge in industrial robotics due to the stochastic nature of pneumatic actuation and the geometric paradox of mapping circular seams to linear processing heads. This research proposes an integrated mechatronic solution utilizing a synthesized reciprocating linkage and a Jacobian-based coordinate mapping strategy. By replacing stochastic fluid-power with deterministic kinematic synchronization, we enable sub-millisecond control cycles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1 ~\text{ms}$</tex>). Experimental validation in a high-speed sewing application at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,000 \text{RPM}$</tex> demonstrates an 85% reduction in path-tracking error compared to traditional PB systems. This architecture provides a scalable framework for high-throughput, autonomous manufacturing in sectors previously limited by material variability.

title: Human-Centered Explainable AI for Security Enhancement: A Deep Intrusion Detection Framework
authors: Md Muntasir Jahid Ayan, Md. Shahriar Rashid, Tazzina Afroze Hassan, Hossain Md. Mubashshir Jamil, Mahbubul Islam, Lisan Al Amin, Rupak Kumar Das, Farzana Akter, Faisal Quader
abstract: The increasing complexity and frequency of cyberthreats demand intrusion detection systems (IDS) that are not only accurate but also interpretable. This paper presented a novel IDS framework that integrated Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to enhance transparency in deep learning models. The framework was evaluated experimentally using benchmark dataset NSL-KDD demonstrating superior performance compared to traditional IDS and black-box deep learning (DL) models. The proposed approach combined Convolutional Neural Network (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network for capturing temporal dependencies in traffic sequences. Our DL results showed that both CNN and LSTM reached 0.99 for accuracy whereas LSTM outperformed CNN at macro average precision, recall and F-1 score. For weighted average precision, recall and F-1 score, both model scored almost similar. To ensure interpretability, XAI model SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) was incorporated, enabling security analysts to understand and validate model decisions. Some notable influential features were srv_serror_rate, dst_host_srv_serror_rate, and serror_rate for both models as pointed out by SHAP. We also conducted a trust-focused expert survey based on IPIP6 and Big Five personality traits via an interactive user interface (UI) to evaluate the system's reliability and usability. This work highlighted the potential of combining performance and transparency in cybersecurity solutions and recommends future enhancements through adaptive learning for real-time threat detection.

title: Data-Driven Insights & Predictive Modeling in Fitness Tracking
authors: Nabeel M Sheikh, Amit Kumar, Vansh Agarwal
abstract: Heart rate variability (HRV) is increasingly used in wearable-based fitness tracking due to its sensitivity to stress, recovery, and cardiovascular function. This study examined whether body composition metrics (BMI and body fat percentage) collected manually and via wearables are consistent, and whether HRV features offer additional insight into individual health trends. A review of prior literature highlighted both the promise and challenges of integrating HRV into predictive modeling, especially regarding individual variability and external influences. The purpose of this study was to assess the reliability of wearable data and evaluate whether HRV varies systematically with body composition. Using datasets from Kaggle and Harvard Dataverse, we analyzed biometric, activity, and HRV metrics from 35 participants. Results showed that wearable-reported body composition data closely matched manual entries, supporting their use in health tracking. However, no strong associations were found between body composition and HRV features. In contrast, HRV varied significantly over time, while BMI remained stable, highlighting HRV's utility for personalized monitoring. These findings support the reliability of wearable sensors and reinforce the value of HRV as a dynamic health indicator for individualized wellness insights. These findings suggest that HRV may serve as a more responsive and individualized metric for real-time health feedback, offering practical applications for adaptive fitness programs and stress management interventions.

title: Design of a Solar-Based Energy-Efficient IoT-PWM-Peltier Operated Advanced HVAC-AHU Cold Storage Without VFD Technique
authors: Sourish Mitra, Sandip Roy, Rajesh Bose
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Proper preservation of daily consumables like fruits and vegetables is essential for human health, as it helps extend their shelf life while maintaining their nutritional value without compromising taste. On this occasion, cold storage plays a vital role for preserving these consumables and making them available during the off-season. Conventional HVAC system of cold storage suffers from high energy consumption due to Compressor-Condenser-Evaporator based AHU mechanism, which provides non-adjustable cooling at a constant speed, even demand becomes low, manual controls that lack real-time adaptability, high operational costs, inconsistent accuracy, high carbon emissions and overcooling issues. Modern VFD equipped AHU can solve above problems for some extent but still suffers from solar un-compatibility with grid dependency, high harmonic distortion, more power consumption due to inclusion of external inverters for converting 3 phase-grid AC to DC that added energy loss and EMI filtering required for reducing high EMI, high maintenance cost, moderated carbon emission with heat production, non remote accessibility etc. In this proposal, solar powered IoT based PWM-peltier operated advanced AHU cooling unit is designed for achieving better environment sustainability with less carbon emission, implementing CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> -VOC-excess humidity removal strategy and energy efficient dynamic PWM cooling intensity adjustment without measuring airflow rate.

title: Design and Simulation of Terahertz Antennas for Biomedical Sensing: A Comprehensive Performance Analysis
authors: Arpita Santra, Maitreyi Ray Kanjilal, Moumita Mukherjee, Puja Paul
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) antennas are increasingly recognized for their transformative potential in biomedical applications, owing to their ability to interact with biological tissues without inducing ionizing effects. This paper presents a comprehensive analytical study of THz antenna systems, emphasizing design architectures, modelling methodologies, simulation protocols, and key performance indicators such as gain, radiation efficiency, and specific absorption rate (SAR). Optimization strategies are explored through advanced material engineering and the integration of machine learning techniques to enhance antenna performance and adaptability. The relevance of these antennas is demonstrated across diverse biomedical domains, including high-resolution medical imaging, non-invasive diagnostic sensing, and wearable health monitoring systems, highlighting their pivotal role in nextgeneration healthcare technologies.

title: Emergency Department Forecasting: COVID-19 Impact on Machine Learning Models
authors: Adán Somoza López, Hugo Álvarez-Chaves, Ana Carrero Fernández, Maria D. R-Moreno
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: COVID-19 disrupted emergency department (ED) arrival patterns, creating challenges for traditional forecasting models used in hospital resource planning. This study analyzes ED arrival data from two Spanish hospitals to assess how the pandemic period affects Machine Learning (ML) model performance and identify optimal forecasting strategies. We applied multiple ML techniques (XGBoost, Random Forest, Elastic Net) and timeseries algorithms (ARIMA, Prophet) using both daily and work shift temporal aggregations, incorporating exogenous calendar variables, including holidays and academic schedules. Models were trained on pandemic-inclusive and post-pandemic periods to evaluate the impact of including disrupted historical data. Results demonstrate that models trained exclusively on postpandemic data consistently outperform those including pandemic data, achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> improvements of up to 0.13 for 4-month forecasts. Work shift aggregation significantly enhanced performance compared to daily aggregation (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0. 9 2 vs 0. 6 4 for best models). SHAP analysis revealed that recent patient arrivals and calendarrelated features serve as the most important predictors across all scenarios. These findings indicate that excluding pandemic data from training datasets and employing finer temporal aggregation substantially improve ED arrival forecasting accuracy, providing practical benefits for hospital operations and resource allocation.

title: From Prototype to Production: A Checklist for Production-Ready AI Focused on Data Quality, Monitoring, and Human Oversight
authors: Femi Oke, Anjum Razzaque, Abdullahi Ibiyeye
abstract: Many AI projects face failure since the data is messy, since AI is not monitored properly, or possibly end-users are not involved enough. Building such a prototype appears straightforward, whereas a production-ready system is more challenging. For instance, Machine Learning (ML) projects fail to transition from promising prototypes to reliable products due to issues with data quality, lack of continuous monitoring, or insufficient human oversight. We present a clear checklist of the current research gap for developing “production-ready” AI systems across three critical areas: data quality, continuous monitoring, and human oversight. Our work offers best practices for each area, using clean, unbiased, and representative data, monitoring models for performance and data changes, and incorporating human-in-the-loop for safety and accountability. Such a checklist helps identify early risks, mitigate technical problems, and promote trust in AI. Case studies and references help us establish best practices to bridge the gap from prototype to production research.

title: Cough Detection for Smart-Home Health Monitoring: Room-Scale Edge Acoustic Sensing with Mel-Spectrogram Classification
authors: Jelan Womack, Fariha Alam, Muhammad Hassan Tanveer, Yusun Chang, Razvan Cristian Voicu
abstract: Smart homes are increasingly studied as sensing platforms that can deliver context-aware services and health monitoring without requiring constant user attention. Cough is a clinically informative symptom spanning respiratory infection, asthma exacerbation, allergies, and exposure to environmental irritants; changes in cough activity can support earlier screening, improve symptom timeline fidelity, and enable longitudinal studies outside the clinic. This paper presents a room-scale cough detection sensor for smart-home health monitoring that emphasizes low-latency, privacy-aware edge processing. The system continuously estimates sound level from a far-field four-channel directional microphone array, triggers short-window recording only after a threshold crossing (74 dB), converts captured audio into a mel spectrogram, and classifies events using an ESC-50-based label space with cough as the target class. All feature extraction and inference were performed locally on an embedded edge compute module, enabling data minimization and avoiding cloud upload by default. We evaluated the feasibility at 1 m, 3 m, and 5 m from the sensor with uncovered and covered-mouth coughs. Across 18 staged trials, the prototype achieved 77.8 % cough classification accuracy, with stable performance at 1-3 m and degradation at 5 m. Misclassifications were driven primarily by transient-event similarity and dataset/domain mismatch between curated training clips and in-room acoustics. Beyond feasibility, we provide a deployment-oriented analysis covering privacy threats, event semantics for smart-home integration, and a road map toward research-grade validation, including binary cough-vs-non-cough modeling, in-situ fine-tuning, adaptive triggering, and multi-participant evaluation.

title: A Virtual Braille Keyboard Prototype for Accessible Text Input on Smartphones
authors: Larissa Braga De Carvalho, Afonso Ueslei Da Fonseca, Thamer Horbylon Nascimento, Eduardo Amorim, Juliana Felix, Fabrizzio Soares
abstract: Text input on smartphones remains a significant challenge for users with visual impairments, particularly regarding privacy. Despite advances in accessibility, private and efficient text entry is a critical challenge for blind users. While tactile interfaces are promising, hey require robust software infrastructure. This paper presents the architectural redesign and implementation of Braille Écran, transforming into a native Android Input Method Service (IME). Unlike previous version restricted to messaging, this update enables systemwide Braille input. We introduce a novel Connect interaction mode to complement standard touch, leveraging a heuristicbased confirmation algorithm. By establishing an extensible, open-source framework, this work overcomes legacy limitations and provides the necessary foundation for future research into hybrid tactile-digital interfaces. Main contributions include the IME migration, a dual-mode interaction model, and a modular extensible architecture.

title: Control-Aware Fault Propagation and Latency Analysis in Distributed Automotive Electrification Architectures for ADAS Applications
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
abstract: Distributed automotive electrification architectures increasingly support ADAS functions through tightly coupled sensor-actuator loops that depend on deterministic timing. In practice, faults can propagate across power and communication domains, and latency can accumulate across zonal ECUs, gateways, and electrification controllers, affecting closed-loop stability and safe actuation. This paper presents a control-aware architectural analysis of fault propagation and latency behavior in distributed automotive electrification architectures for ADAS applications. The study evaluates how timing margin erosion, synchronization drift, and cascading faults influence control robustness across representative centralized, modular, and hybrid topologies. A system-level framework is used to relate architectural decisions (partitioning, fault containment boundaries, and communication determinism) to control-relevant outcomes such as actuation delay and stability margin degradation. The paper then provides practical design recommendations—including hierarchical control partitioning, latency-aware fault management, and hybrid communication strategies—to improve resilience under realistic timing and fault-propagation constraints. The results support the need for control-centric architectural evaluation as electrified platforms scale toward higher automation ADAS use cases

title: MIRA: Mutual Information-Based Reconstruction Attacks on Split Learning
authors: Linbo Zhou
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Split learning (SL) has been proposed as a lightweight alternative to federated learning, as it retains raw data on the client side and exchanges only intermediate activations with the server. Nevertheless, recent studies have shown that such smashed features may still reveal sensitive information about the underlying inputs. In this work, we introduce a new reconstruction attack against SL from an informationtheoretic perspective, termed the Mutual-Information Guided Reconstruction Attack (MIRA). MIRA first trains a substitute client on an auxiliary dataset and aligns its representation space with that of the victim client by maximizing a contrastive lower bound on the conditional mutual information between their activations. After establishing this cross-domain feature coupling, a reconstruction network is trained offline to invert substitute features to the input space and is subsequently applied directly to the recorded smashed data. Experiments on MNIST and CIFAR10 using VGG16 demonstrate that MIRA achieves high-quality reconstructions across different cut points and remains effective under limited auxiliary data, underscoring substantial privacy risks in SL under an honest-but-curious server.

title: A Hybrid-Domain Detector for OTFS Signals
authors: Che-Wei Chang, Chun-Hao Yen, Pei-Jin Su, Hsiang-Heng Yeh, Kuei-Chiang Lai
abstract: This paper presents a reduced-complexity iterative detector for zero-padding orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) systems. In each iteration, the detector first cancels the inter-symbol interference (ISI), followed by removing the inter-Doppler interference (IDI) via a hybrid-domain decision-feedback equalizer (DFE). The DFEś feedforward and feedback filters are efficiently designed to operate in the delay-time and delay-Doppler domains, respectively, by exploiting the OTFS signal structures. Computer simulations over the third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) extended vehicular A (EVA) channel with a velocity of 500 km/hr and perfect channel estimation show that, for 16-QAM, the proposed detector outperforms the maximal-ratio combining detector by about 1.18 dB at a bit-error rate of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> , at the cost of about 102.1% increase in complexity.

title: Low-complexity Adaptive Fourier Integral Method for Large Aperture Horizontal Linear Arrays at Low Frequency and Low SNR Environments
authors: Cong Wang, Xionghou Liu, Yuyuan Zhou, Yixin Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Weak signal detection poses a fundamental challenge in passive sonar measurement systems, particularly under low-frequency and low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a low-complexity adaptive Fourier integral method (LCA-FIM), which introduces an innovative signal processing framework designed to enhance the measurement capabilities of large-aperture horizontal linear arrays (HLAs). The core innovation is an adaptive window selection strategy: multiple pre-designed windows with different noise gains and sidelobe levels are applied to FIM, generating several groups of weighted spatial spectra. Subsequently, an adaptive selection rule extracts the minimum output at each azimuth. This strategy enables LCA-FIM to effectively optimize the trade-off between key performance metrics, achieving high noise gain, high angular resolution and low sidelobe levels simultaneously. Numerical simulations demonstrate that LCA-FIM shows a better weak signal detection performance under strong-interference and low-SNR conditions, when compared to conventional beamformer (CBF), FIM, weighted FIM (WFIM), and diagonally loaded minimum variance distortionless response beamformer. Experimental validation using real ocean data further confirms the effectiveness of the proposed LCA-FIM method in clutter-rich, noisy, low-frequency and low-SNR environments. While validated in a passive sonar context, the proposed LCA-FIM is a general-purpose measurement method applicable to a wide range of array-based sensing system requiring weak signal analysis.

title: A Highly Reliable Drive Circuit for Solid-State High-Voltage Pulse Generators
authors: Yuanhao Liu, Lei Wang, Hongyu Dai, Jun Huang, Liuxia Li, Yi Liu
abstract: As the application scenarios of solid-state high-voltage pulse generator (SSHVPG) continue to expand, the requirements for reliability are getting higher and higher. Inside the SSHVPG, the drive circuit is the key to its reliability. In this article, a novel, highly reliable drive circuit is proposed for the SSHVPG that provides both overvoltage protection and overcurrent protection. The drive circuit uses active clamping to suppress the turn-off overvoltage, and the overcurrent protection circuit based on the thyristor is applied to ensure that the semiconductor switches can turn off quickly and stably when overcurrent appears. The simulation model was established to analyze the protection mechanism in different situations, and it was found that the overcurrent protection and overvoltage protection do not work independently, but there is a mechanism of mutual influence, and both of them work together to complete the overcurrent protection of the semiconductor switch. Prototype experimental results verify the feasibility and practicality of this drive circuit.

title: 3D Gaussian Splatting Texture Editing via Single Modified Image
authors: Hanul Baek, Dohae Lee, Kyumin Kim, In-Kwon Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Recently, 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a powerful technique for reconstructing high-quality, photorealistic 3D representations of real-world scenes. However, editing 3DGS remains more challenging than mesh-based approaches as it lacks explicit geometry and requires view-consistent updates across Gaussians. Previous approaches have relied primarily on text-driven generative models for 3D Gaussian editing, limiting direct control over specific visual appearances. In this study, we propose a texture editing framework for 3DGS that leverages only a single user-modified image. Our approach maintains coherence across multiple views while accounting for the corresponding lighting adjustments in the edited region. We introduce three key techniques to achieve this: (1) aligned edit propagation, which transfers local edits from the reference view to other viewpoints; (2) mask-based filtering, which restricts modifications to relevant Gaussians and prevents unintended changes; and (3) opacity-based selection, which identifies Gaussians with the most significant visual impact on the edited texture. Through both qualitative and quantitative evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets, we demonstrate that our method achieves more precise and spatially controllable 3DGS editing than existing techniques. We expect these techniques to pave the way for more intuitive 3D Gaussian Splatting editing pipelines and inspire future research.

title: VTBSAE: Provably Secure Verifier-based Password Authenticated Key Exchange for IIoT
authors: Mingping Qi
abstract: Password-authenticated key exchange (PAKE) has gained widespread adoption in prominent platforms such as iCloud to provide realistic security assurance. It offers robust security by enabling mutual authentication and strong session key establishment between two parties via a low-entropy password. This paper presents an efficient verifier-based PAKE protocol called VTBSAE (Verifier-based Two-Basis Simultaneous Authentication of Equals) based on the well-known SAE protocol, aiming to provide light, reliable, and scalable security assurances for Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). The primary technological innovation lies in the utilization of two distinct bases over the underlying group to implement the Hash-to-Curve (H2C) function employed in SAE, and transform the symmetric SAE protocol into a verifier-based PAKE protocol, thereby enhancing its resilience against server compromise attacks. Moreover, adopting the widely accepted BPR (Bellare-Pointcheval-Rogaway) security model with further considering the perfect forward secrecy, VTBSAE’s security is formally proved under the standard CDH security assumption. This solid foundation provides VTBSAE with certain security advantages over some existing verifier-based PAKE protocols whose security proofs rely on some stronger security assumptions. VTBSAE can work over both the multiplicative and elliptic curve additive groups. Particularly, in the elliptic curve setting, VTBSAE requires no reliance on the H2C function, which is often challenging to implement securely over the commonly used elliptic curve parameters such as the secp256r1. Therefore, VTBSAE demonstrates significant improvements in ease of secure implementation compared to the existing verifierbased PAKE protocols that rely on the H2C function.

title: Distributed Estimator-Based Adaptive Fuzzy Prescribed-Time Consensus Control for Nonlinear Multiagent Systems
authors: Jiguang Peng, Zhi Liu, C. L. Philip Chen, Yun Zhang
abstract: This article investigates the problem of the prescribed-time consensus control for nonlinear multiagent systems under performance constraints and actuator attacks. First, a distributed output-feedback-based prescribed-time estimator is designed for each follower. This enables the followers to precisely estimate the leader’s signal. Subsequently, to achieve the control objective with prescribed performance, a prescribed-time performance function is constructed. Integrated with an asymmetric barrier Lyapunov function approach, it guarantees that asymmetric time-varying performance constraints are satisfied within the preset time. Furthermore, fuzzy logic systems are adopted to approximate unknown nonlinear functions. In addition, to handle the issue of unknown control directions, Nussbaum gain functions are incorporated into the controller design. The proposed controller ensures the boundedness of all signals in the closed-loop system, while simultaneously guaranteeing that the tracking errors remain within the prescribed performance bounds. Finally, simulation results demonstrate the advantages and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Prefrontal Hierarchical Control of Corticomuscular Coherence in Stroke
authors: Jasper I. Mark, Rachana Gangwani, Justin Riddle, Jessica M. Cassidy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Stroke recovery involves reorganization of large-scale brain networks, yet most neurophysiological biomarkers assess either motor or cognitive function in isolation. To address this limitation, we introduce phase-modulated corticomuscular coherence (pmCMC), a novel metric that quantifies how prefrontal delta-phase activity modulates beta-band corticomuscular coherence between the primary motor cortex and peripheral muscle. This approach integrates cross-frequency and corticomuscular coupling, offering a dynamic index of cognitive-motor interaction. We applied pmCMC to a cohort of individuals with hemiparetic stroke who performed a precision grip task during high-density EEG and EMG recording at admission and discharge from an inpatient rehabilitation facility. Our findings reveal that pmCMC increases across rehabilitation and positively relates to corticostriatal but not corticospinal tract injury. Additionally, higher pmCMC at admission was positively associated with greater gains in motor coordination, functional independence, and cognitive status. These results position pmCMC as hybrid neurophysiological measure of cognitive-motor integration relevant to post-stroke recovery and encourages further investigation of its prognostic utility, causal mechanisms, and applicability across diverse motor tasks and clinical populations.

title: Modeling and Capacity Analysis of UAV-Assisted Three-tier Heterogeneous Wireless Networks
authors: Su Zhan, Xiaorong Zhu, Xiaohua Qiu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Sudden disasters frequently disrupt terrestrial cellular infrastructure, severely degrading network capacity in affected regions and necessitating the rapid restoration of uplink connectivity for rescue and monitoring operations. To address this challenge, we propose a systematic capacity analysis framework for a three-tier emergency wireless network comprising a device access layer, an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) relay layer, and a backhaul layer. First, within the access layer, heterogeneous ground devices are modeled using a Poisson point process (PPP). We analyze the uplink transmissions under non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), deriving the coverage probability and ergodic capacity while accounting for imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). Next, in the relay layer, multiple concurrent flows are scheduled via time-frequency resource allocation across end-to-end UAV links, incorporating practical communication constraints and multihop packet-loss effects. To balance throughput and fairness, we introduce a joint resource allocation scheme that maximizes a novel satisfaction function. In the backhaul layer, the capacity between UAVs and base stations (BSs) is characterized under space-division multiple access (SDMA) with directional beamforming, explicitly modeling the impact of antenna-orientation jitter at both the UAV and BS sides. Finally, the overall end-to-end system capacity is formulated as the bottleneck capacity among the three tiers. Analytical and simulation results validate the accuracy of the proposed theoretical model. Furthermore, comparative evaluations demonstrate that the proposed resource allocation scheme for the UAV relay layer significantly improves allocation fairness among concurrent flows relative to conventional baselines.

title: VTVC: Vignetting Correction Based on Variant Transformer and Spatial Self Attention
authors: Zhuang He, Haoxin Hu, Haowen Zheng, Hai-Miao Hu
abstract: Vignetting correction is an essential part for image signal processor. It is important for imaging systems to get high quality images. Traditional vignetting correction methods are almost based on traditional hand-crafted features, which usually perform poorly in complex scenario. Recently, deep learningbased vignetting correction methods have been widely studied. However, the receptive fields of these methods are relatively small, which is difficult for them to estimate the overall vignetting of the image accurately. In this paper, we proposed a deep learning-based vignetting correction model which includes three major components: variant transformer structure, multiple layer upsampling and spatial self attention. The variant transformer structure is construct by replacing the multi-layer perceptron of traditional transformer with a structure extractor module, so that to improve the receptive fields for vignetting estimation. The multiple layer upsampling and spatial self attention are used to improve the resolution and preserve the image details, thereby improving the accuracy of vignetting correction. Additionally, due to the lack of vignetting correction dataset, we introduced a method to get vignetting images through capturing the real world, and construct the first thousand-level vignetting correction dataset. Besides, we also proposed a data augmentation method based on the optical principle of vignetting. Through training on the large amount of real-world vignetting dataset, the proposed vignetting correction network can be trained accurately. Many experiments have been done on the conducted dataset. Through the comparison with the state-of-the-art algorithms, it has been verified that the proposed vignetting correction network demonstrates the best performance.

title: GaussianFlow SLAM: Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM Guided by GaussianFlow
authors: Dong-Uk Seo, Jinwoo Jeon, Eungchang Mason Lee, Hyun Myung
abstract: Gaussian splatting has recently gained traction as a compelling map representation for SLAM systems, enabling dense and photo-realistic scene modeling. However, its application to monocular SLAM remains challenging due to the lack of reliable geometric cues from monocular input. Without geometric supervision, mapping or tracking could fall in local-minima, resulting in structural degeneracies and inaccuracies. To address this challenge, we propose GaussianFlow SLAM, a monocular 3DGS-SLAM that leverages optical flow as a geometry-aware cue to guide the optimization of both the scene structure and camera poses. By encouraging the projected motion of Gaussians, termed GaussianFlow, to align with the optical flow, our method introduces consistent structural cues to regularize both map reconstruction and pose estimation. Furthermore, we introduce normalized error-based densification and pruning modules to refine inactive and unstable Gaussians, thereby contributing to improved map quality and pose accuracy. Experiments conducted on public datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior rendering quality and tracking accuracy compared with stateof- the-art algorithms. The source code is available at: https: //github.com/url-kaist/gaussianflow-slam.

title: A Water-Controlled Dielectric Resonator Antenna with Unilateral and Omnidirectional Pattern Reconfigurability
authors: Xuan-Ji Wu, Yu-Tao Liang, Zhe Chen, Tao Yuan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a reconfigurable rectangular dielectric resonator antenna (RDRA) whose radiation patterns can be switched by controlling the liquid water. By loading a high-permittivity material to modify the feeding source, specific resonant modes of the RDRA are excited, thereby achieving reconfigurable radiation patterns. Based on this concept, a 3D-printed microfluidic container is integrated at the bottom of the RDRA. By injecting water into different sections of the container, reconfigurable control of the radiation pattern is realized. To validate the proposed idea, a prototype is fabricated and measured, demonstrating excellent overall performance, including five distinct patterns with an overlapping bandwidth of 20% while maintaining a compact size. The demonstrated performance indicates that the proposed antenna is well-suited for integrated sensing and communication applications.

title: Zr-Based Contacts to n-Type AlN with Improved Performance for Power and High-Temperature Electronics
authors: Zhongyunshen Zhu, Hridibrata Pal, Aditya Dev Varma, Masataka Imura, Hironori Okumura, Tomás Palacios
abstract: This work reports improved ohmic contacts on n-type AlN using a Zr/Al/Mo/Au metal stack with contact resistivities of ~10-3 Ω⋅cm2 at 25°C and ~10-5 Ω⋅cm2 at 500°C, which are over one and two orders of magnitude lower than Ti-based contacts, respectively. Temperature-dependent current-voltage characteristics reveal thermionic-field emission-dominated transport at the Zr/AlN interface, leading to improved conductivity and ohmic behavior above 400°C. Record-low specific on-resistance (Ron,sp) is achieved in AlN Schottky barrier diodes using Zr-based cathode compared to previously reported values at comparable breakdown voltages. The devices reveal 10× enhanced Baliga Figure of Merit at 300°C and 500°C due to significantly lowered Ron,sp < 50 mΩ⋅cm2.
